title: Robust Dense Chinese Vegetables Yangzhou Fried Rice
description: A tangy and dense Chinese dish from Zhejiang, featuring clotted cream and a mirepoix base, making it a vegetarian friendly option. This adventurous recipe is a twist on the classic Yangzhou fried rice.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium high heat.
Add the diced onions, carrots, and celery and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet or wok, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir in the soy sauce, salt, and pepper, and cook for 2 minutes.
Push the rice mixture to one side of the skillet or wok, then add a little more oil if necessary.
Add the clotted cream to the other side of the skillet or wok and stir until it is heated through.
Mix the clotted cream with the rice mixture, then stir in the chopped scallions.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove the skillet or wok from the heat and serve the Yangzhou fried rice hot.
